Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: List all issues under the expense auto-categorizer project using linear_list_issues (project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer" or query: "expense auto-categorizer" or similar). Expected findings: ENG-209 (policy ingestion, Samira, todo), ENG-211 (pilot monitoring, Samira, in_progress), ENG-212 (edge case coverage/schema validation, Dmitri, in_progress), ENG-216 (full audit, Dmitri, in_progress/urgent), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, Lena, in_progress), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, Anh, in_progress), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, Samira, todo), ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection, Dmitri, todo). All due dates are in February–March 2026 and have passed.
OE 2: Read comments on the edge case coverage ticket (issue_edge_cases_backlog / ENG-212) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_edge_cases_backlog"). Expected finding: Dmitri implemented 6 schema validation tests on March 20, 2026 (test_config_field_types, test_required_fields, test_config_matches_source, test_exclusion_entries_valid, test_limit_values_positive, test_no_silent_fallback). Remaining items include edge case tests for categorization, vendor abbreviations, and internet setup vs monthly. Ticket still shows "in_progress" with no further update since March 20.
OE 3: Read comments on the pipeline hardening ticket (issue_pipeline_hardening / ENG-217) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). Expected finding: Dmitri posted the full 47-expense audit results here on March 20, 2026 (39 correct, 5 false rejections, 3 false approvals, $885 financial exposure). No comment from Lena confirming pipeline hardening completion itself. Ticket still shows "in_progress".
OE 4: Read comments on the dashboard type validation ticket (issue_dashboard_type_validation / ENG-218) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_dashboard_type_validation"). Expected finding: NO comments exist on this ticket. Anh was supposed to add type validation to the config editor. Due date was March 21, 2026. Ticket shows "in_progress" but has no visible activity, making it silently stalled.
OE 5: Read comments on the launch readiness checklist ticket (issue_launch_readiness_checklist / ENG-219) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_launch_readiness_checklist"). Expected finding: NO comments exist on this ticket. Samira was assigned it, due March 21, 2026. State is still "todo", never started.
OE 6: Read comments on the pilot monitoring ticket (issue_pilot_monitoring / ENG-211) using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pilot_monitoring"). Expected findings: Samira's false rejection incident report (March 16), Mina's two-incident report (March 16), Emeka's false approvals report (March 16), Lena's root cause analysis (March 17), Marcus's financial controls request (March 17), David's client impact assessment (March 17), Elena's CEO directive (dated March 17 in body, posted March 18 UTC), and Samira's auto-decisions disabled confirmation (March 18). This provides the full incident timeline context.
OE 7: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or "pipeline hardening" or "schema validation" or similar) to cross-reference Linear ticket statuses against internal team discussions. Expected findings: engineering channel discussions from February and March about architecture decisions, Chloe's requirements relay, and remediation coordination. Allows the agent to verify whether there has been post-March activity on the remediation work.
OE 8: Search emails using search_emails (query: "expense" or "auto-categorizer" or "remediation" or "pipeline" or similar) to find email updates about remediation progress. Expected findings: Samira's original ops requirements request to Chloe, pilot launch notifications to Mina and Emeka, the full incident email chain (Emeka's false approvals, Mina's false rejections, Marcus's financial escalation, David's client impact, Elena's directive), Samira's remediation plan, and Samira's audit results email.
OE 9: Search Linear for the duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "duplicate" or "receipt" or "hash" or similar). Expected finding: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220), title "Stipend duplicate detection — receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", state "todo", assigned to Dmitri, due date March 25, 2026 (overdue by over a month), priority P1.
OE 10: Read comments on the duplicate receipt detection ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_stipend_duplicate_detection"). Expected finding: one comment from Dmitri (March 15, 2026) providing context and stating "I will begin the Layer 1 implementation (hash dedup) on Monday morning since Lena has confirmed that the S3 hash data is already accessible." No subsequent comments confirming work started. Ticket remains in "todo" state despite being overdue.
OE 11: Search Linear for the invoice attachment safety check ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "safety" or "template" or similar). Expected finding: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, title "Invoice email template safety checks — prevent attachment mismatches", state "backlog", assigned to Lena, due April 18, 2026 (past due), project: none (NOT under the expense auto-categorizer project). This ticket is separate from the ExpenseBot project.
OE 12: Read comments on the invoice safeguard ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2"). Expected findings: Lena's April 6 deferral comment, Samira's April 6 acknowledgment, Samira's April 15 escalation ("Assign an owner today", "treat this as incident follow-up"), Marcus's April 15 comment about the Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure, Marcus's April 22 comment calling it a "control deficiency" and requesting active ownership. Despite two escalations, no comment confirms a new owner or any implementation progress.
OE 13: List Airtable bases using airtable_list_bases to find the MoveOps Operations base (appMoveOpsOps001).
OE 14: List tables in the MoveOps Operations base using airtable_list_tables (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001") to find the Stipend Transactions table (tblStipends00001).
OE 15: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Vectral records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Stipend Transactions", field_name: "Account", value: "Vectral Systems"). Expected findings: Maria Santos standing desk $550 (Approval Status: "Rejected", Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"), James Okafor ergonomic keyboard tray $82 (Approval Status: "Rejected", Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"), Lisa Park mesh WiFi system $130 (Approval Status: "Rejected", Review Notes: "Pending manual correction"). All three are still uncorrected as of April 26.
OE 16: Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
OE 17: Look up Marcus Thorne's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus Thorne"). Expected finding: marcus.thorne@moveops.com.
OE 18: Add a comment (via linear_create_comment) on each stalled or unresolved ticket noting the current blocker and what needs to happen next. Candidate tickets include:
- issue_edge_cases_backlog (ENG-212): 6 schema validation tests done but edge case categorization tests incomplete since March 20
- issue_pipeline_hardening (ENG-217): no confirmation from Lena that pipeline hardening is complete
- issue_dashboard_type_validation (ENG-218): no activity or comments since creation; silently stalled since March 18
- issue_launch_readiness_checklist (ENG-219): still "todo", never started, due March 21
- issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (ENG-220): still "todo", overdue since March 25, no progress since Dmitri's March 15 plan
- linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (invoice safeguard): still unassigned/backlog despite two escalations; needs owner and fastest shippable stopgap
OE 19: Create a new Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) to track resolving the three uncorrected Vectral stipend rejections before relaunch. Parameters: team: "team_engineering", title: related to "Resolve pending Vectral stipend corrections before ExpenseBot relaunch" (or similar), description: referencing Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130 still showing "Rejected" with "Pending manual correction" in Airtable. This OE is conditional on OE 15 confirming the corrections have not been made.
OE 20: Send an email (via send_email) from samira.tariq@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com, CC marcus.thorne@moveops.com, with a subject related to "Engineering Status Update" (or similar) and body covering: (a) where the expense tool relaunch stands across all remediation tickets, (b) the state of the three pending Vectral stipend corrections that remain uncorrected, (c) whether the duplicate receipt detection work has started, and (d) what engineering is doing about the invoice attachment safeguard gap.
OE 21: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in the #engineering channel (channel_id: "C003") with a payload summarizing the current engineering status and noting that all open items need to be closed out before end of week.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_get_issue, or linear_list_comments) to look up engineering ticket statuses.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls (querying 'expense auto-categorizer', 'stipend', 'invoice email template safety', 'attachment mismatch', etc.) and linear_list_projects calls throughout the trajectory. Linear tools were clearly used to look up ticket statuses.
✓Run #2The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls (querying 'expense', 'stipend', 'invoice', 'categoriz', 'edge case', etc.), used linear_list_comments on multiple issue IDs, and retrieved a project's issues via linear_list_issues with project='proj_expense_autocategorizer'. Linear tools were clearly used to look up engineering ticket statuses.
✓Run #3The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls (querying 'expense', 'stipend', 'pipeline', 'validation', 'invoice email', 'Vectral', 'attachment mismatch', etc.), linear_list_comments calls on specific issue IDs, and linear_list_projects calls. The Linear tool was clearly used to look up engineering ticket statuses.
✓Run #4The agent used mcp__mcp_9349cbbb__linear_list_issues multiple times (querying 'expense auto-categorizer', 'expense categorizer', 'stipend', 'expense', 'invoice email template safety', 'attachment mismatch') and also used linear_list_projects and linear_list_issues with a project filter, satisfying the requirement to look up engineering ticket statuses via Linear tools.
✓Run #5The agent made multiple linear_list_issues calls (querying 'expense auto-categorizer', 'stipend', 'expense', 'pipeline', etc., with team='team_engineering' and project='proj_expense_autocategorizer'), linear_list_comments calls on specific issue IDs, and linear_get_user calls. Linear tools were clearly and extensively used.
✓Run #6The agent made multiple calls to `mcp__mcp_33505484__linear_list_issues` with various queries (expense, stipend, invoice attachment, etc.) and also used `linear_list_projects` and `linear_update_issue`. The tool was clearly used to look up engineering ticket statuses.
2 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_list_tables) to look up stipend transaction records.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called airtable_list_bases to discover available bases, then airtable_list_tables to enumerate tables, and then airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_name 'Stipend Transactions', field_name 'Account', value 'Vectral' to pull the actual stipend records.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_bases to discover bases, then airtable_list_tables to discover tables, and then called airtable_search_records on the Stipend Transactions table. Airtable tools were used to look up stipend transaction records.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001' and table_name 'Stipend Transactions' to look up stipend records, including searches for Maria Santos, James Okafor, Lisa Park, Danielle Osei, Kevin Tran, and a search by Account='Vectral'.
✓Run #4The agent called mcp__mcp_9349cbbb__airtable_list_bases, then mcp__mcp_9349cbbb__airtable_list_tables, and then mcp__mcp_9349cbbb__airtable_search_records on the 'Stipend Transactions' table to look up Vectral stipend records, satisfying this criterion.

✓Run #6The final response identifies ENG-218 as 'Unknown status, no confirmation from Anh', and the Linear ticket update notes 'Anh committed to implementing frontend validation on 3/18 but no confirmation of completion found in Slack or elsewhere. Ticket still marked In Progress.' This correctly identifies it as silently stalled.

outcome
